name: Las Merindades — circular desde Puentedey por Cogullos y Leva
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Merindad de Valdeporres traverse a diverse landscape shaped by extensive forests of beech, pine, and oak, and the Nela and Engaña river valleys. The region's terrain is influenced by the Montes del Somo, offering varied elevations and unique rock formations. This area provides a rugged backdrop for mountain biking, with routes winding through natural arches and ancient woodlands. Best mountain bike trails around Merindad De Valdeporres The most popular mountain bike trail is Embalse del Ebro y Hayedo de Carrales — circular desde Villabáscones de Bezana, a 20.9 miles (33.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 53 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the Ebro reservoir and ancient beech forests. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Las Merindades — circular desde Puentedey por Cogullos y Leva, a moderate 17.1 miles (27.5 km) path. It offers varied terrain through the Merindades region, passing by the natural rock arch of Puentedey. Local mountain bikers also love the Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey – Puentedey loop from Brizuela, a 30.2 miles (48.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain and past the iconic Puentedey Natural Bridge, often completed in about 5 hours 22 minutes. Mountain biking around Merindad De Valdeporres is defined by extensive forests, river valleys, and varied elevations. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Merindad De Valdeporres are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 260 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Merindad De Valdeporres's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Merindad de Valdeporres?
A: There are over 60 mountain bike trails in Merindad de Valdeporres, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. These routes traverse diverse landscapes, including extensive forests, river valleys, and unique rock formations.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Merindad de Valdeporres?
A: The terrain in Merindad de Valdeporres is highly varied, influenced by the Montes del Somo and traversed by the Nela and Engaña rivers. You'll find routes winding through expansive forests of beech, pine, and oak, as well as along river valleys and past dramatic rock formations. The region offers a rugged backdrop with varied elevations, from gentle paths to more challenging climbs.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Merindad de Valdeporres offers several easy mountain bike trails, with 5 routes specifically categorized as easy. These are ideal for beginners or families looking for a more relaxed ride. The region's varied terrain ensures there are options for all abilities, including accessible sections of the 'Camino Natural Santander-Mediterráneo' which follows parts of the former railway.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many mountain bike routes in Merindad de Valdeporres are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the moderate route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1104405553/las-merindades-circular-desde-puentedey-por-cogullos-y-leva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Merindades — circular desde Puentedey por Cogullos y Leva&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied terrain through the Merindades region.

Q: What interesting natural landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Merindad de Valdeporres is rich in natural landmarks. You can cycle past the remarkable &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3900715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey&lt;/a&gt;, a geological wonder carved by the Nela River. The region also features the dramatic Cascada de la Mea (Mea Waterfall), accessible via a signposted trail, and extensive forests like the centennial oak forest of Robredo.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, the region blends natural beauty with historical sites. You might encounter the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3900706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Bernabé of Ojo Guareña&lt;/a&gt; or the Dolmen de Busnela, a prehistoric megalithic monument. Sections of the former Santander-Mediterráneo railway, including the Engaña Tunnel, have been repurposed as a 'Vía Verde' (Greenway), offering a unique historical cycling experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Merindad de Valdeporres?
A: The climate in Merindad de Valdeporres, influenced by its altitude and the Cantabrian mountains, results in abundant spring and autumn rainfall. This fosters lush vegetation and a unique landscape, making spring and autumn particularly scenic times for mountain biking. Summers are generally pleasant, while winters can be colder with potential for snow at higher elevations.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Merindad de Valdeporres?
A: The mountain bike routes in Merindad de Valdeporres are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the extensive forests, and the variety of routes catering to different skill levels, from moderate to difficult.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, Merindad de Valdeporres offers 18 difficult mountain bike routes. These trails often feature significant elevation changes and more technical sections. An example of a challenging route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31466745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey – Puentedey loop from Brizuela&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 48 km with substantial elevation gain.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by the Cascada de la Mea waterfall?
A: Yes, the Cascada de la Mea (Mea Waterfall) is a notable natural feature in the region. While primarily known as a hiking trail (PRC-BU 36), mountain bikers can find routes in the vicinity that allow for a visit to this dramatic waterfall, which winds through a narrow canyon and a magnificent forest of gall oaks.

Q: Are there any multi-adventure parks or family activities nearby?
A: Yes, for those looking for additional activities, Valterria Adventure Park is located in the region. It offers a multi-adventure experience with circuits of bridges, zip lines, and interpretive centers, catering to all ages and even providing nighttime activities, making it a great option for families.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that explore the Ebro reservoir?
A: Yes, you can find mountain bike trails that explore the scenic Ebro reservoir. A popular moderate route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1100353781/embalse-del-ebro-y-hayedo-de-carrales-circular-desde-villabascones-de-bezana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Ebro y Hayedo de Carrales — circular desde Villabáscones de Bezana&lt;/a&gt;, which also takes you through ancient beech forests.
tips: A fun trail suitable for all levels that runs along the banks of the Nela River as it passes between Puentedey and Brizuela.
Small Burgos town with an interesting popular architecture. The town is protected by mountains. The church dedicated to San Miguel Arcángel stands out.
Fun trail through the woods near the town of Leva.

This is considered one of the most beautiful towns in Spain and for good reason. It is crossed by the Río Nela and where the houses are built above a natural cave that has created the passage of the river by making a hole in the stone itself. You can not miss it!

waypoints.name: Church of San Miguel Arcángel of Quintanilla Valdebodres
